edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Stage 1 pretraining of OLMo 3 32B was performed on Dolma 3 Mix, a 6T-token pretraining mix; the 32B model used 5.5T tokens from the mix (released as dolma3_mix-5.5T-1125).

evidence.excerpt: We first train Olmo 3 Base on Dolma 3 Mix, our 6T token pretraining data mix.
evidence.explanation: Identifies Dolma 3 Mix as the Stage 1 pretraining corpus for OLMo 3 Base.

evidence.location: Model card, Training Details / Stage 1 Initial Pretraining
evidence.excerpt: Dataset: allenai/dolma3_mix-5.5T-1125 — Tokens: 5.50 Trillion
evidence.explanation: 32B model card lists the precise pretraining dataset and 5.5T token count.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Stage 2 midtraining of OLMo 3 32B used 100B tokens sampled from the Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix; the 32B specifically used two 100B ingredients trained with different seeds and merged via model souping before stage 3.

evidence.excerpt: During this midtrain stage, we use 100B high-quality tokens sampled from a brand new data pool we introduce in this work, Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.
evidence.explanation: Identifies Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix as the Stage 2 midtraining corpus.

evidence.excerpt: Dataset Name: allenai/dolma3_dolmino_mix-100B-1125 — 100B tokens (used twice)
evidence.explanation: Confirms exact dataset and that 32B used the mix twice (two midtraining runs that were souped).

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Stage 3 long-context extension of OLMo 3 32B trained on 100B tokens from Dolma 3 Longmino Mix (34% long-context PDFs + 66% midtraining data) to extend context to 65,536 tokens; final 32B is a soup of the last three long-context checkpoints.

evidence.excerpt: We mix 34% long-context data with 66% high-quality short-context data sampled from Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix, and train using this mix for an additional 50B tokens for Olmo 3 7B and 100B tokens for Olmo 3 32B
evidence.explanation: Specifies Stage 3 long-context training and the 100B token budget for the 32B variant.

evidence.excerpt: Dataset: allenai/dolma3_longmino_mix-100B-1125 — Tokens: 100B
evidence.explanation: Confirms long-context dataset for the 32B base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Common Crawl web pages (104 dumps with cutoff Dec 31, 2024) form the largest sub-corpus of Dolma 3 Mix at 4.51T tokens (76.1% of the mix). OLMo 3 reused Resiliparse-extracted data from DCLM-pool where available, and applied Resiliparse extraction to remaining dumps. Common Crawl (HQ subset) also reappears as 22.5% of the Dolmino midtraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: Common Crawl — Web pages — 8.14T pool — 4.51T (76.1%) in 6T mix … We start with 104 dumps from the CommonCrawl corpus, with a cutoff date of December 31, 2024.
evidence.explanation: Table 4 enumerates Common Crawl as the dominant pretraining sub-corpus; §3.4.1 documents the 104 CC dumps and DCLM-pool reuse.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: olmOCR science PDFs (academic documents OCR-extracted with olmOCR v0.1.49–0.1.53) contributed 805B tokens (13.6%) to the Dolma 3 Mix pretraining pool. A high-quality subset (240B pool / 5% of the Dolmino midtraining mix) was reused in midtraining, and the same pool seeded the long-context Longmino mix.

evidence.excerpt: olmOCR science PDFs — Academic documents — 805B (13.6%) in 6T mix
evidence.explanation: Table 4 lists olmOCR science PDFs as a pretraining sub-corpus with token contributions.

evidence.location: Section 3.4.2 (Preparing our olmOCR science PDFs Data Pool), p.16
evidence.excerpt: We curate a novel dataset of academic PDFs, replacing our previous use of peS2o … We process all PDFs using the first version of olmOCR (Poznanski et al., 2025a).
evidence.explanation: Documents the construction of olmOCR science PDFs as a new academic-PDF corpus.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Stack-Edu (Allal et al., 2025) — an educational-quality filtered curation of GitHub repositories from the-stack-v2 — contributed 409B code tokens (6.89%) to Dolma 3 Mix. A FIM-transformed variant (StackEdu FIM) also appeared in the Dolmino midtraining mix at 10B tokens.

evidence.excerpt: For code data, we use Stack-Edu (Allal et al., 2025), an improved curation of GitHub repositories from the-stack-v2 dataset (Lozhkov et al., 2024) with additional filtering for educational programming content.
evidence.explanation: Names Stack-Edu as the code source for OLMo 3 pretraining.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OLMo 3 included the arXiv subset of Proof-Pile-2 (Azerbayev et al., 2023) — papers with LaTeX, sourced via RedPajama with cutoff April 2023 — contributing 50.8B tokens (0.86%) to Dolma 3 Mix. Used to preserve mathematical LaTeX notation.

evidence.excerpt: As in OLMo 2, we include arXiv documents from the Proof-Pile-2 dataset (Azerbayev et al., 2023), which in turn are from the RedPajama dataset (Together AI, 2023) and have a cutoff date of April 2023.
evidence.explanation: Identifies the Proof-Pile-2 arXiv subset as a pretraining math/science source.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: FineMath 3+ (Allal et al., 2025) — a Common-Crawl-derived math web corpus filtered to FineMath quality score ≥3, cutoff September 2024 — contributed 152B tokens (2.56%) to Dolma 3 Mix. Replaced OpenWebMath used in OLMo 2.

evidence.excerpt: we replace our previous use of OpenWebMath (Paster et al., 2023) with FineMath (Allal et al., 2025)… We include all documents that have a quality score of at least 3 (out of 4)
evidence.explanation: Documents adoption of FineMath (3+ subset) as the primary math web corpus for pretraining.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Wikipedia and Wikibooks sources (English and Simple editions, cutoff March 2023) processed via Dolma (Soldaini et al., 2024) contributed 2.51B tokens (0.04%) of encyclopedic knowledge to Dolma 3 Mix.

evidence.excerpt: we include the Wikipedia and Wikibooks sources from Dolma (Soldaini et al., 2024) as base sources of encyclopedic knowledge. These are both the "English" and "Simple" editions of Wikipedia and Wikibooks with a cutoff date of March 2023.
evidence.explanation: Names Dolma as the source for OLMo 3's Wikipedia/Wikibooks data.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: TinyMATH MIND, a newly synthesized dataset of conversational English discussions of MATH-style problems, contributed 898M tokens (~0.9%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: For each of the 7500 examples in the MATH training set, we generate 100 new, similar problems… two flavors of conversational English discussing these solutions (TinyMATH-MIND).
evidence.explanation: Defines TinyMATH-MIND and its inclusion in the Dolmino midtraining mix per Table 5.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: TinyMATH PoT (Python-code program-of-thought solutions to synthesized MATH-style problems) contributed 241M tokens (~0.24%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: We then create Python code solutions to the newly for each problem (TinyMATH-PoT)
evidence.explanation: Defines TinyMATH-PoT and its inclusion per Table 5.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: CraneMath, a permissively-licensed reproduction of the SwallowMath rewriting recipe applied to FineMath4+, contributed 5.62B tokens (5.63%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: we generate an independent reproduction of SwallowMath by rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt, using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a) for generation. We denote this new mix as CraneMath, which yields 5.6B tokens
evidence.explanation: Defines CraneMath and its midtraining inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MegaMatt, a permissively-licensed reproduction of MegaMath-Web-Pro-Max — applying the same MegaMath filter to MegaMath-Web data after June 2023 and rewriting with Qwen3 — contributed 1.73B tokens (1.73%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023, apply filtering as in Megamath-Web-Pro-Max, and rewrite it using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a). This yields 3.88B tokens of high-quality data, which we refer to as MegaMatt.
evidence.explanation: Defines MegaMatt and its midtraining inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Stage 2 reused two prior subsets of OLMo 2's Dolmino Mix 1124: (1) the entire 10.7B-token Dolmino Math subset (10.7% of the Dolmino mix) — described as 'reuse of previously-introduced data' — and (2) a Dolmino 1 Flan subset (5.0B tokens, 5%).

evidence.excerpt: Dolmino-1 math: We include the entirety of the 10.7B-token OLMo 2 Dolmino Mix Math subset.
evidence.explanation: Documents reuse of OLMo 2 Dolmino Math; Table 5 also lists 'Dolmino 1 Flan' as 5.0B (5%).

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: For Stage 2 midtraining, OLMo 3 included a Stack-Edu variant (StackEdu FIM, 10B tokens / 10% of Dolmino) in which 50% of documents were transformed via the StarCoder2 fill-in-the-middle (FIM) infilling procedure, with quality filtering using HuggingFace SmolLM language-specific stack-edu-classifiers. This is a transformation of the original Stack-Edu corpus (which is also a separate trained_on edge for Stage 1 pretraining).

evidence.excerpt: Stack-Edu (FIM) We include a modified version of Stack-Edu, in which 50% of documents reflect fill-in-the-middle (FIM) transformation via the infilling procedure from StarCoder2 (Lozhkov et al., 2024).
evidence.explanation: Documents the FIM transformation of Stack-Edu used in midtraining.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: CraneCode, a permissively-licensed reproduction of SwallowCode (two-stage style+optimize rewrite of Python code from the-stack-v2-smol), contributed 10B tokens (10%) of Python code to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: As with our math datasets, we find strong performance from the SwallowCode dataset, and generate a permissively-licensed recreation for use in our midtraining… apply the SwallowCode two-stage rewriting pipeline … This yields 18.8B tokens of high-quality python code.
evidence.explanation: Defines CraneCode and its midtraining inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Reddit-to-Flashcards, synthesized by prompting GPT-4o-mini to rewrite academic-subreddit submission/comment pairs into multiple-choice QA pairs across 7 task formats, contributed 5.9B tokens (5.9%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: We first identify a subset of academically-relevant subreddits, and then use GPT 4o-mini to rewrite submission-comment pairs from those subreddits into multiple-choice QA pairs.
evidence.explanation: Defines Reddit-to-Flashcards construction and inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Wiki-to-RCQA, synthesized by prompting Qwen2.5 32B Instruct on Wikipedia passages to generate reading-comprehension QA pairs, contributed 3.0B tokens (3.0%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: We collect Wikipedia passages and prompted Qwen2.5 32B Instruct to generate QA pairs based on these passages
evidence.explanation: Defines Wiki-to-RCQA construction and inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OLMo 3 included the 'diverse QA pairs' synthetic subset of NVIDIA's Nemotron-CC dataset (Su et al., 2025a) — 5.0B tokens (5%) of Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining. Other Nemotron synth subsets ('distill', 'extract knowledge', 'knowledge list', 'wrap medium') were tested but not used.

evidence.excerpt: We include the "diverse QA pairs" synth subset of the Nemotron CC dataset (Su et al., 2025a)
evidence.explanation: Identifies the specific Nemotron-CC subset used.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Math Meta-Reasoning, a newly-introduced synthetic dataset of meta-cognitive reasoning traces over math problems generated by GPT-4.1 and o4-mini, contributed 381M tokens (0.38%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining. Targets 7 cognitive capabilities from Kargupta et al. (2025) modeled after the Pandalla-Math annotation schema.

evidence.excerpt: we prompt GPT-4.1 and o4-mini to generate thinking traces for each capability-targeted task.
evidence.explanation: Documents the generators of meta-reasoning traces and Pandalla-Math modeling basis.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Code Meta-Reasoning, the code-domain counterpart to Math Meta-Reasoning, contributed 459M tokens (0.46%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining. Same GPT-4.1 / o4-mini generation pipeline; sourced from coding problems including Hendrycks et al. (2021a).

evidence.excerpt: synthetically augmented existing math (Luo et al., 2025a; Moshkov et al., 2025) and code (Li et al., 2023a; Hendrycks et al., 2021a; Ahmad et al., 2025) problems with detailed annotations
evidence.explanation: Documents code source for meta-reasoning generation; Table 5 lists 'Code Meta-Reasoning' separately.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Program-Verifiable data — programmatically generated tasks whose answers a Python verifier can deterministically check; thinking traces distilled from GPT-4.1 and o4-mini, then filtered for correctness — contributed 159M tokens (0.16%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: we 1) programmatically generate these problems, 2) distill thinking traces from GPT-4.1 and o4-mini models, and 3) finally filter those for correctness using an output verifier (Python programs).
evidence.explanation: Defines program-verifiable data construction.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OMR Rewrite FullThoughts, a light rewrite of the OpenMathReasoning dataset by GPT-4.1 (improving clarity, flow, LaTeX formatting while preserving reasoning), contributed 850M tokens (0.85%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: This is a light rewrite of the OpenMathReasoning dataset, instructing GPT-4.1 to edit items for clarity, flow, and formatting (e.g., converting to LaTeX) while preserving all reasoning, explanations, and thoughts of the original.
evidence.explanation: Defines OMR Rewrite FullThoughts as a GPT-4.1 rewrite of OpenMathReasoning.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: QWQ Reasoning Traces — a filtered subset of externally available QwQ reasoning trace data — contributed 1.87B tokens (1.87%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining as one of multiple existing thinking-trace sources adopted with quality filtering.

evidence.excerpt: Thinking (synth) — QWQ Reasoning Traces — 1.87B (1.87%)
evidence.explanation: Table 5 lists QWQ Reasoning Traces as a midtraining sub-corpus (filter/light-transform of external data).

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: General Reasoning Mix — a filtered/aggregated set of external reasoning trace datasets covering math, code, sciences, humanities, and puzzles — contributed 1.87B tokens (1.87%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: Thinking (synth) — General Reasoning Mix — 1.87B (1.87%)
evidence.explanation: Table 5 lists General Reasoning Mix; §3.5.2 frames the curation as filtering existing thinking-trace datasets.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Gemini Reasoning Traces — a filtered set of externally-released Gemini-generated reasoning traces — contributed 246M tokens (0.25%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: Thinking (synth) — Gemini Reasoning Traces — 246M (0.25%)
evidence.explanation: Table 5 lists Gemini Reasoning Traces as a midtraining sub-corpus.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Llama-Nemotron Reasoning Traces — a filtered subset of NVIDIA's Llama-Nemotron post-training dataset — contributed 1.25B tokens (1.25%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: Thinking (synth) — Llama Nemotron Reasoning Traces — 1.25B (1.25%)
evidence.explanation: Table 5 lists Llama Nemotron Reasoning Traces as a midtraining sub-corpus.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OpenThoughts2 Reasoning Traces — a filtered subset of the open-thoughts/OpenThoughts2-1M reasoning dataset — contributed 1.25B tokens (1.25%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.excerpt: Thinking (synth) — OpenThoughts2 Reasoning Traces — 1.25B (1.25%)
evidence.explanation: Table 5 lists OpenThoughts2 traces as a midtraining sub-corpus.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: A processed sample of Tülu 3 SFT data (with double-newline message concatenation rather than chat-template special tokens) contributed 1.1B tokens (1.1%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining; included an expanded set beyond the published Lambert et al. (2024) release.

evidence.excerpt: We sample instruction data from the SFT set from Tülu 3. Compared to dataset released by Lambert et al. (2024), we lightly process these data
evidence.explanation: Documents Tulu 3 SFT inclusion in midtraining mix.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: STEM-Heavy Crawl, an in-house web crawl (Sep 12, 2024 – Jun 3, 2025) seeded from manually-curated high-value scientific/educational domain lists and quality-classifier-filtered to the top ~2.83% of crawled content, contributed 4.99B tokens (5%) to Stage 2 Dolmino midtraining.

evidence.location: Section 3.5.2 (High quality web and PDF data), p.25
evidence.excerpt: Stem-heavy crawl We also create a separate high-quality web collection, crawled between September 12, 2024 and June 3rd, 2025 using our in-house crawler.
evidence.explanation: Defines STEM-Heavy Crawl and its midtraining inclusion.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Long-context natural olmOCR PDFs at 8K-16K, 16K-32K, and 32K-64K length buckets (LC-s2pdf) form the natural-document portion of Stage 3 Longmino long-context training. gzip-compressibility filtering applied (top/bottom 20% removed).

evidence.location: Table 11 (Composition of Dolma 3 Longmino Mix), p.30
evidence.excerpt: olmOCR PDFs 8K-16K … 16K-32K … 32K-64K …
evidence.explanation: Table 11 lists the LC-s2pdf length-bucketed long-context PDF sub-corpora.

evidence.excerpt: The backbone of our long-context data pool is scientific PDFs scraped from the web and processed by olmOCR.
evidence.explanation: Confirms LC-s2pdf as the natural long-context document backbone.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: LC-s2pdf + synth CWE (Common Word Extraction) — synthetic aggregation tasks injected into 32K-64K PDFs by prompting OLMo 2 Instruct 32B to generate QA pairs whose answers are exact unigram occurrence counts — contributed 1.94B tokens (3.88%) to Stage 3 Longmino long-context training.

evidence.excerpt: CWE (Common Word Extraction) We prompt OLMo 2 Instruct with 5 commonly occurring single-word noun phrases in the partition, and ask the model to generate diverse QA pairs that require the answer to be the exact number of times each unigram occurs
evidence.explanation: Defines CWE synthetic augmentation; Table 11 lists tokens.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: LC-s2pdf + synth REX (Rewriting EXpressions) — synthetic aggregation tasks where OLMo 2 Instruct 32B rewrites passages around salient noun phrases into one of 12 stylistic vignettes (summary, dialogue, flashcards, quiz, debate, ELI5, etc.) — contributed 6.08B tokens (12.2%) to Stage 3 Longmino long-context training.

evidence.excerpt: REX (Rewriting EXpressions) For each noun phrase and corresponding snippets, we prompt OLMo 2 Instruct to generate an aggregation task matching one of the following 12 vignettes
evidence.explanation: Defines REX synthetic augmentation; Table 11 lists tokens.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Qwen3-32B served as the sole generator model for two midtraining sub-corpora that fed OLMo 3 32B's Stage 2 weights: (1) CraneMath — rewrote FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt, yielding 5.6B tokens; (2) MegaMatt — rewrote post-Jun-2023 MegaMath-Web data following the MegaMath-Web-Pro-Max filter, yielding 3.88B tokens.

evidence.excerpt: using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a) for generation. We denote this new mix as CraneMath … rewrite it using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a). This yields 3.88B tokens of high-quality data, which we refer to as MegaMatt.
evidence.explanation: Documents Qwen3-32B as generator for both CraneMath and MegaMatt.

evidence.excerpt: for CraneMath we used Qwen3-32B for all data generation
evidence.explanation: Confirms exclusivity of Qwen3-32B as the CraneMath generator.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Qwen2.5 32B Instruct generated the Wiki-to-RCQA midtraining sub-corpus by being prompted on Wikipedia passages to produce reading-comprehension QA pairs (3.0B tokens / 3% of Dolmino).

evidence.excerpt: prompted Qwen2.5 32B Instruct to generate QA pairs based on these passages
evidence.explanation: Identifies Qwen2.5 32B Instruct as the Wiki-to-RCQA generator.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: GPT-4o-mini generated the Reddit-to-Flashcards midtraining sub-corpus by rewriting academic-subreddit submission/comment pairs into multiple-choice QA pairs across 7 task formats (5.9B tokens / 5.9% of Dolmino).

evidence.excerpt: use GPT 4o-mini to rewrite submission-comment pairs from those subreddits into multiple-choice QA pairs.
evidence.explanation: Identifies GPT-4o-mini as the Reddit-to-Flashcards generator.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: GPT-4.1 served as a generator for three midtraining sub-corpora: (1) Math Meta-Reasoning thinking traces (with o4-mini), (2) Code Meta-Reasoning thinking traces (with o4-mini), (3) Program-Verifiable thinking traces (distilled with o4-mini, then verified by Python programs), and (4) OMR Rewrite FullThoughts (light rewrite of OpenMathReasoning for clarity, flow, LaTeX formatting).

evidence.excerpt: we prompt GPT-4.1 and o4-mini to generate thinking traces … distill thinking traces from GPT-4.1 and o4-mini models … instructing GPT-4.1 to edit items for clarity, flow, and formatting
evidence.explanation: Documents GPT-4.1's three generation roles in midtraining data construction.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: o4-mini served as a co-generator with GPT-4.1 for the Math Meta-Reasoning, Code Meta-Reasoning, and Program-Verifiable midtraining sub-corpora, generating thinking traces for capability-targeted tasks.

evidence.excerpt: we prompt GPT-4.1 and o4-mini to generate thinking traces for each capability-targeted task … distill thinking traces from GPT-4.1 and o4-mini models
evidence.explanation: Documents o4-mini as a thinking-trace generator alongside GPT-4.1.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OLMo 2 Instruct 32B was the sole generator for both synthetic long-context augmentation tasks in Stage 3: CWE (Common Word Extraction; counting unigram occurrences) and REX (Rewriting EXpressions; 12 stylistic vignettes). Together these synthetic augmentations contributed ~16% of the Longmino mix.

evidence.location: Section 3.6.2 (Generation pipeline / Synthetic aggregation tasks), p.33
evidence.excerpt: While we experimented with several closed and open language models, we ultimately use OLMo 2 Instruct 32B for all generations.
evidence.explanation: Documents OLMo 2 Instruct 32B as the exclusive long-context synth generator.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Gemma 3 12B (instruction-tuned) was used as a PII classifier on the first page of every olmOCR science PDF — judging whether the document contained sensitive standalone PII or linked sensitive info to an individual — as part of the multi-stage model-based PII filtering pipeline that gated entry of olmOCR PDFs into Dolma 3 Mix pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: First we classify documents using a prompt to Gemma 3 12B (Gemma 3 Team, 2025) on the first page of each document to determine if they contain any sensitive standalone PII
evidence.explanation: Identifies Gemma 3 12B as the first-stage PII classifier in the olmOCR pipeline.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Gemma 3 4B (instruction-tuned) was used as a document-type classifier on the first 5,000 characters of every olmOCR science PDF — generating flags consumed by rule-based PII filtering that gated entry of olmOCR PDFs into Dolma 3 Mix pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: Next, we use Gemma 3 4B on the first 5,000 characters of each document to arrive at a set of flags describing the type of document.
evidence.explanation: Identifies Gemma 3 4B as the document-type classifier in the PII pipeline.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: FineMath4+ — the higher-quality 9.6B-token subset of FineMath — was the source dataset whose content Qwen3-32B rewrote (per the SwallowMath prompt) to construct CraneMath, the 5.62B-token math midtraining sub-corpus included in Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.

evidence.excerpt: we generate an independent reproduction of SwallowMath by rewriting FineMath4+ with the SwallowMath prompt, using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a) for generation.
evidence.explanation: Documents FineMath4+ as the source content rewritten into CraneMath.

evidence.excerpt: Starting with Finemath-4plus, for each example, we use Qwen3-32B to rewrite the example by prepending a system message containing the prompt:
evidence.explanation: Confirms FineMath-4plus as the source rewritten into CraneMath.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MegaMath-Web (the post-June-2023 portion, with the MegaMath-Web-Pro-Max filter applied) was the source dataset whose content Qwen3-32B rewrote to construct MegaMatt, contributing 3.88B tokens (1.73%) to Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.

evidence.excerpt: we collect the Megamath-Web-Pro data occurring after June 2023, apply filtering as in Megamath-Web-Pro-Max, and rewrite it using Qwen3 (Yang et al., 2025a).
evidence.explanation: Documents MegaMath-Web as the source content rewritten into MegaMatt.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: the-stack-v2-train-smol (Lozhkov et al., 2024) — specifically its Python subset — was the source code corpus whose content was filtered (syntax/linter) and then rewritten via the SwallowCode two-stage style+optimize pipeline to construct CraneCode (10B Python tokens in Dolmino).

evidence.excerpt: we source data from the Python subset of the-stack-v2-smol, then filter for syntax errors and filter based on linter outputs.
evidence.explanation: Documents the-stack-v2-smol Python subset as the source content rewritten into CraneCode.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OpenMathReasoning (Moshkov et al., 2025) was the source dataset whose content GPT-4.1 rewrote into OMR Rewrite FullThoughts — light edits for clarity, flow, and LaTeX formatting while preserving original reasoning — contributing 850M tokens (0.85%) to Dolma 3 Dolmino Mix.

evidence.excerpt: This is a light rewrite of the OpenMathReasoning dataset, instructing GPT-4.1 to edit items for clarity, flow, and formatting
evidence.explanation: Documents OpenMathReasoning as source content rewritten by GPT-4.1.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Transitive: FineMath (used as 152B-token math sub-corpus of Dolma 3 Mix pretraining) was annotated by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct on a 3-point and then 5-point quality scale; those annotations trained the FineMath classifier (multilingual-e5-small) that filtered Common-Crawl pages into the FineMath release. Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct's annotations therefore filtered training tokens that entered OLMo 3's pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: we replace our previous use of OpenWebMath (Paster et al., 2023) with FineMath (Allal et al., 2025), a subset of Common Crawl documents that contain mathematical educational content … We include all documents that have a quality score of at least 3 (out of 4), according to the FineMath classifier.
evidence.explanation: Establishes that OLMo 3 trained on FineMath quality-classifier-filtered documents.

evidence.excerpt: Fine-grained 5-point quality assessment using Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct; retained only scores ≥3
evidence.explanation: FineMath card identifies Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct as the annotator that produced the quality labels used by the filter.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Transitive: Stack-Edu (used as 409B code tokens in Dolma 3 Mix pretraining and as the source for the StackEdu-FIM midtraining sub-corpus) was filtered by 15 language-specific classifiers (StarEncoder-based) trained on synthetic educational-quality annotations generated by Llama-3-70B-Instruct. Llama-3-70B-Instruct's annotations therefore filtered code tokens that entered OLMo 3's pretraining mix.

evidence.excerpt: For code data, we use Stack-Edu (Allal et al., 2025), an improved curation of GitHub repositories from the-stack-v2 dataset (Lozhkov et al., 2024) with additional filtering for educational programming content.
evidence.explanation: Establishes that OLMo 3 trained on Stack-Edu's classifier-filtered code.

evidence.excerpt: 15 language-specific classifiers trained using StarEncoder model as the base; Synthetic annotations generated by Llama3-70B-Instruct
evidence.explanation: Stack-Edu card identifies Llama-3-70B-Instruct as the annotator producing labels for the educational-quality classifier.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-2-1124-32b-instruct
edges.description: OLMo 2 32B Instruct (which OLMo 3 used as the long-context synth generator) was post-trained from the OLMo-2-1124-32B base via Tülu-3-style SFT, DPO, and RLVR.

evidence.excerpt: OLMo 2 32B Instruct is a post-trained variant of the OLMo-2 32B base model, which has undergone supervised finetuning on an OLMo-specific variant of the Tülu 3 dataset, further DPO training on this dataset, and final RLVR training
evidence.explanation: Establishes OLMo-2-1124-32B as the base from which OLMo-2-1124-32B-Instruct is post-trained.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-2-1124-32b
edges.description: OLMo 2 32B base was pretrained on OLMo-Mix-1124 (3.9T tokens from DCLM, Dolma, Starcoder, and Proof Pile II).

evidence.excerpt: Pre-training occurs on OLMo-Mix-1124, a collection of 3.9 trillion tokens sourced from DCLM, Dolma, Starcoder, and Proof Pile II
evidence.explanation: Names the pretraining mix for OLMo 2 32B base.

evidence.location: OLMo 2 technical report (Walsh, Soldaini, Groeneveld et al., 2025)
evidence.excerpt: OLMo-Mix-1124 … 3.9T tokens
evidence.explanation: Confirms OLMo-Mix-1124 as the OLMo 2 pretraining corpus.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-2-1124-32b
edges.description: OLMo 2 32B base was midtrained on the Dolmino Mix 1124 (the OLMo 2 midtraining mix) introduced via late-stage curriculum training.

evidence.excerpt: specialized data mix called Dolmino Mix 1124, which significantly improves model capabilities across many downstream task benchmarks when introduced via late-stage curriculum training
evidence.explanation: Names Dolmino Mix 1124 as OLMo 2's midtraining mix.

edges.subject: qwen/qwen2.5-32b-instruct
edges.description: Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (the Wiki-to-RCQA generator for OLMo 3) was instruction-tuned from the Qwen2.5-32B base.

evidence.excerpt: This model is initialized from Qwen/Qwen2.5-32B (the base pretrained model). The instruction-tuned 32B version is built on top of this foundation.
evidence.explanation: HF card establishes Qwen2.5-32B as the base for Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct.

edges.subject: meta-llama/llama-3.1-70b-instruct
edges.description: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (used as the FineMath quality annotator) was post-trained from the Llama-3.1-70B base released by Meta.

evidence.excerpt: Llama 3.1 instruction tuned text only models … are optimized for multilingual dialogue use cases
evidence.explanation: HF card frames the Instruct variant as instruction-tuned on the same Llama 3.1 70B base architecture/weights.

edges.subject: meta-llama/meta-llama-3-70b-instruct
edges.description: Llama-3-70B-Instruct (used as the Stack-Edu educational-quality annotator) was instruction-tuned from the Meta-Llama-3-70B base.

evidence.excerpt: Meta Llama 3 70B Instruct … instruction tuned text only model
evidence.explanation: HF card establishes Meta-Llama-3-70B as the base for the Instruct variant.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: The CraneMath construction methodology — rewriting carefully-curated mathematical web data (FineMath4+) with a high-quality LLM — was directly borrowed from SwallowMath (Fujii et al., 2025) with the modification that Qwen3-32B replaced the Llama suite as the generator (since SwallowMath's Llama-rewritten data carried license restrictions). OLMo 3 corroborated SwallowMath's gains via microanneals before reproducing the recipe.

evidence.excerpt: The recently published SwallowMath dataset (Fujii et al., 2025) demonstrates the potential of rewriting already finely-curated naturally-occurring mathematical web data … Because SwallowMath comes with additional license restrictions—having been generated with the Llama suite of models—we generate an independent reproduction of SwallowMath
evidence.explanation: Documents SwallowMath as the methodological inspiration for CraneMath with an explicit substitution of Qwen3-32B for Llama.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: The CraneCode construction methodology — sourcing Python from the-stack-v2-smol, filtering by syntax/linter, and applying a two-stage style-augment then code-optimize rewriting pipeline — was directly borrowed from SwallowCode (Fujii et al., 2025), with the substitution of an open-source generator to permit permissive licensing.

evidence.excerpt: As with our math datasets, we find strong performance from the SwallowCode dataset, and generate a permissively-licensed recreation for use in our midtraining. Like Fujii et al. (2025), we source data from the Python subset of the-stack-v2-smol, then filter for syntax errors and filter based on linter outputs. Then, we apply the SwallowCode two-stage rewriting pipeline
evidence.explanation: Documents SwallowCode as the methodological inspiration for CraneCode.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: The MegaMatt construction methodology — applying MegaMath's filtering pipeline to MegaMath-Web data and rewriting with a high-quality LLM — was directly borrowed from MegaMath-Web-Pro-Max (Wang et al., 2025), with the substitution of Qwen3-32B for Llama and a temporal cutoff (post-June-2023 data only) to enable open re-release.

evidence.excerpt: Similar to SwallowMath, Megamath-Web-Pro-Max (Wang et al., 2025) applies Llama rewrites to naturally-occurring mathematical web text … However, in order to use this dataset, we re-generate it using open source models.
evidence.explanation: Documents MegaMath-Web-Pro-Max as the methodological inspiration for MegaMatt.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: The Stage 3 Longmino synthetic-augmentation pipeline — modifying long PDFs by injecting synthetically generated aggregation tasks (CWE/REX) at randomly-sampled intervals — was 'directly inspired by' CLIPPER (Pham et al., 2025).

evidence.excerpt: Directly inspired by CLIPPER (Pham et al., 2025), we modify a portion of our science PDF pool by injecting synthetically generated aggregation tasks at randomly sampled intervals.
evidence.explanation: Documents CLIPPER as the explicit methodological inspiration for the Longmino synthetic augmentation.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OLMo 3's Stage 3 long-context synthetic data pipeline shares similarities with Qwen 2.5 1M (Yang et al., 2025b) — specifically the approach of bootstrapping long-context training data via aggregation tasks generated from extracted document statistics.

evidence.excerpt: Our approach also shares similarities with Qwen 2.5 1M (Yang et al., 2025b).
evidence.explanation: Documents Qwen 2.5 1M's long-context recipe as a related methodological reference.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OLMo 3 borrowed multiple methodological ingredients from DCLM (Li et al., 2024a) for its web-data pipeline: Resiliparse-based HTML→text extraction (with reuse of DCLM-pool's pre-extracted data), DCLM's heuristic filtering pipeline (with minor modifications), and DCLM's choice of OpenHermes-2.5 + ELI5 as positive training examples for the fastText quality classifier (with DCLM-RefinedWeb negatives). The first round of conditional mixing was also performed on DCLM-Baseline before transferring weights to OLMo 3's bespoke web pool.

evidence.location: Section 3.4.1 (Text extraction / Heuristic filtering / Topic and quality classification), p.14–17
evidence.excerpt: Following DCLM (Li et al., 2024a), we remove HTML artifacts and extract the semantic text from WARC files using Resiliparse … Our process closely follows that of DCLM (Li et al., 2024a) … Following DCLM (Li et al., 2024a), we use OpenHermes-2.5 (Teknium, 2023) and ELI5 (Fan et al., 2019) as positive training examples
evidence.explanation: Documents multiple specific DCLM-borrowed methodological choices.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: The annotation schema for the Math/Code Meta-Reasoning midtraining datasets — capturing 'problem classification', 'difficulty analysis', 'solution approaches', 'common pitfalls', and 'verification methods' — was modeled after the Pandalla-Math dataset.

evidence.excerpt: detailed annotations such as 'problem classification', 'difficulty analysis', 'solution approaches', 'common pitfalls', and 'verification methods', modeled after the Pandalla-Math dataset.
evidence.explanation: Documents Pandalla-Math as the schema source for meta-reasoning annotations.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Gemma 3 4B was used as the reference language model in the LongPpl filter ablation: OLMo 3 computed LongPpl over 10B tokens of Dolma 3 Longmino Mix using Gemma 3 4B as the reference model, comparing 4K vs 128K context windows. Multiple LongPpl-based filter variants were evaluated against the gzip filter; none outperformed gzip, so LongPpl was not used in the final long-context recipe.

evidence.excerpt: We compute LongPpl over 10B tokens of Dolma 3 Longmino Mix using Gemma 3 4B (Gemma 3 Team, 2025) as the reference model … none of these possibilities outperform the gzip filter, so we do not use this for the final run.
evidence.explanation: Documents Gemma 3 4B as the reference model in a long-context filter ablation that did not make the final recipe.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: OlmoBaseEval is OLMo 3's bespoke base-model evaluation suite — 43 tasks (4× OLMo 2's count) organized into MC STEM, MC Non-STEM, GenQA, Math, Code, and Code FIM clusters, plus a Held-out set (MMLU Pro, DeepMind Math, LBPP, BBH). Used as the primary development metric across pretraining and midtraining for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: The resulting OlmoBaseEval consists of a Base Easy suite for making development decisions using small compute budgets … and a Base Main suite for development decisions for the final pretraining run and midtraining. … OlmoBaseEval contains 43 tasks
evidence.explanation: Defines OlmoBaseEval as the evaluation suite used for OLMo 3 base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MMLU (Hendrycks et al., 2021b) was used to evaluate OLMo 3 32B base, decomposed in OlmoBaseEval into MMLU STEM (70.8), MMLU Humanities (78.3), MMLU Social Sci. (84.0), and MMLU Other (75.1). MMLU is the headline aggregate base-model knowledge benchmark in Table 13.

evidence.excerpt: MMLU STEM 70.8 … MMLU Humanities 78.3 … MMLU Social Sci. 84.0 … MMLU Other 75.1
evidence.explanation: Reports MMLU sub-cluster scores for OLMo 3 32B base across stages.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MMLU Pro is one of OlmoBaseEval's four held-out benchmarks (intentionally excluded from the development suite to prevent overfitting), evaluated on OLMo 3 32B base. 32B base achieved 49.7 MMLU Pro MC.

evidence.excerpt: we include a Held-out set of 4 benchmarks—MMLU Pro, DeepMind Math, LBPP, and BBH … MMLU Pro MC 49.7
evidence.explanation: Identifies MMLU Pro as a held-out evaluation benchmark and reports the 32B score.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: BIG-Bench Hard (BBH) is one of OlmoBaseEval's four held-out benchmarks, evaluated on OLMo 3 32B base. 32B base achieved 77.6 BBH.

evidence.excerpt: a Held-out set of 4 benchmarks—MMLU Pro, DeepMind Math, LBPP, and BBH … BBH 77.6
evidence.explanation: Identifies BBH as a held-out base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: ARC (multiple-choice variant) was an MC STEM benchmark in OlmoBaseEval; OLMo 3 32B base achieved 94.7 ARC MC.

evidence.excerpt: ARC MC 94.7
evidence.explanation: Reports the OLMo 3 32B base ARC MC score in the base eval table.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: HellaSwag (reading-comprehension variant) was a GenQA benchmark in OlmoBaseEval; OLMo 3 32B base achieved 84.8 HellaSwag RC.

evidence.excerpt: HellaSwag RC 84.8
evidence.explanation: Reports HellaSwag RC for OLMo 3 32B base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: WinoGrande (reading-comprehension variant) was a GenQA benchmark in OlmoBaseEval; reported in Table 3 for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: Winogrande RC
evidence.explanation: Lists WinoGrande RC as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: The MATH benchmark (Hendrycks et al., 2021a; Minerva-style scoring also reported as 'Minerva Math') is the principal math benchmark in OlmoBaseEval and aggregate in Table 13. OLMo 3 32B base scored 43.8 MATH at Stage 1 and 61.4 (Math aggregate) after Stage 3.

evidence.excerpt: MATH 43.8 … Math 61.4 (Stage 3)
evidence.explanation: MATH appears across the base evaluation tables for OLMo 3 32B.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: GSM8K is a benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used both for evaluation and for tracking microanneal performance during data curation. Discussed extensively in §3.5 (e.g., GSM Symbolic improvements after model souping).

evidence.excerpt: GSM8K … 5 and 2 points of improvement in GSM Symbolic relative to the first and second runs
evidence.explanation: GSM8K appears as a key evaluation and tracking benchmark for OLMo 3 32B base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: HumanEval is the headline pass@1 code benchmark in OlmoBaseEval; OLMo 3 32B base achieved 65.8 HumanEval at Stage 2 (and 49.1 at Stage 1 / 7B; the 32B Stage 3 result is reported in Table 13).

evidence.excerpt: HumanEval 65.8
evidence.explanation: Reports HumanEval as a base evaluation benchmark for OLMo 3 32B base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MBPP is a Python coding benchmark in OlmoBaseEval (also extended to the multilingual MT MBPP variant). Used to track code capability improvements across stages and microanneals.

evidence.excerpt: MT MBPP, a translated BPB set for MBPP in 17 code languages
evidence.explanation: MBPP and its multilingual extension are part of OlmoBaseEval.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: BigCodeBench (BCB) is one of the headline aggregate base benchmarks in Table 13; OLMo 3 32B base scored 92.6 (Stage 1) → 95.0 (Stage 2 Soup) → 94.8 (Stage 3) BCB.

evidence.explanation: Reports BigCodeBench scores for OLMo 3 32B base across stages.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MultiPL-E (translations of HumanEval and MBPP into many languages) is part of OlmoBaseEval, reported as MultiPL HumanEval and MultiPL MBPP. OLMo 3 32B base scored 36.0 MultiPL HumanEval at Stage 2.

evidence.excerpt: MultiPL HumanEval 36.0
evidence.explanation: Reports MultiPL-E sub-scores for OLMo 3 32B base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: DROP (and a Gen2MC variant) appears in OlmoBaseEval and was the target of decontamination work in §3.5.3.

evidence.excerpt: DROP Gen2MC MC 87.2 … such as validation or test performance changes in DROP, Minerva, SQuAD
evidence.explanation: DROP is reported as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Natural Questions (Gen2MC variant) was a GenQA benchmark in OlmoBaseEval reported for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: NaturalQs Gen2MC MC … Gen2MC, a multiple-choice version of 5 short-form generative tasks
evidence.explanation: Lists NaturalQs as a Gen2MC base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: SQuAD (and a Gen2MC variant) was a GenQA benchmark in OlmoBaseEval and a focus of decontamination work in §3.5.3.

evidence.excerpt: such as validation or test performance changes in DROP, Minerva, SQuAD … SQuAD Gen2MC
evidence.explanation: Reports SQuAD as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: CoQA (and a Gen2MC variant) was a GenQA benchmark in OlmoBaseEval reported for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: CoQA Gen2MC … CoQA
evidence.explanation: Lists CoQA as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Jeopardy (and a Gen2MC variant) is a GenQA benchmark reported in OlmoBaseEval for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: Jeopardy Gen2MC … Jeopardy
evidence.explanation: Lists Jeopardy as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: Lambada is a GenQA benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: Lambada … LAMBADA
evidence.explanation: Lambada is included in OlmoBaseEval.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: CommonsenseQA (CSQA MC) is a Non-STEM MC benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: CSQA MC
evidence.explanation: Lists CSQA as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: PIQA (MC variant) is a Non-STEM MC benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: PIQA MC … PIQA (MC)
evidence.explanation: Lists PIQA as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: SocialIQA (MC variant) is a Non-STEM MC benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: SocialIQA MC … SocialIQA (MC)
evidence.explanation: Lists SocialIQA as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: SciQ (MC variant) is an MC STEM benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: SciQ MC … SciQ (MC)
evidence.explanation: Lists SciQ as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MedQA (English MC variant) is an MC STEM benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: MedQA MC … MedQA En (MC)
evidence.explanation: Lists MedQA as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: MedMCQA (MC variant) is an MC STEM benchmark in OlmoBaseEval used for OLMo 3 32B base.

evidence.excerpt: MedMCQA MC … MedMCQA (MC)
evidence.explanation: Lists MedMCQA as a base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: AGIEval (English) was used to evaluate the OLMo 3 base evaluation suite (and is shared with the post-training suite).

evidence.excerpt: AGI Eval* (2023) — CoT MC — Acc
evidence.explanation: AGIEval appears in the OLMo 3 evaluation suite.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: GPQA appears in the OLMo 3 base/chat evaluation suite (32B base scored 36–67 GPQA depending on stage; flagged as high variance in Table 16).

evidence.excerpt: GPQA* (2024) — CoT MC — Acc … High variance: GPQA: 1.4798
evidence.explanation: GPQA is part of the OLMo 3 evaluation suite.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: LBPP (Less Basic Python Problems) is one of OlmoBaseEval's four held-out benchmarks (intentionally excluded from the development suite to prevent overfitting).

evidence.excerpt: a Held-out set of 4 benchmarks—MMLU Pro, DeepMind Math, LBPP, and BBH
evidence.explanation: Identifies LBPP as a held-out base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: DeepMind Math is one of OlmoBaseEval's four held-out benchmarks for the OLMo 3 base evaluation.

evidence.excerpt: a Held-out set of 4 benchmarks—MMLU Pro, DeepMind Math, LBPP, and BBH
evidence.explanation: Identifies DeepMind Math as a held-out base evaluation benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: BasicSkills is a new 6-task suite introduced in OlmoBaseEval to isolate basic-skill development (arithmetic, reasoning, coding) during pretraining for OLMo 3 base.

evidence.excerpt: BasicSkills, a set of 6 tasks to isolate the development of skills during pretraining (e.g., basic arithmetic, reasoning, and coding)
evidence.explanation: Defines BasicSkills as part of OlmoBaseEval.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: CruxEval (Gu et al., 2024a) is included in OlmoBaseEval as a code I/O prediction benchmark, separated from the macro-average due to high noise but tracked for the unique capability it measures.

evidence.excerpt: separate some benchmarks from the macro-average, like CruxEval (Gu et al., 2024a), which measures a relevant and unique capability (code input/output prediction)
evidence.explanation: Identifies CruxEval as a tracked OlmoBaseEval benchmark.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: RULER (Hsieh et al., 2024) was the primary metric guiding OLMo 3's long-context recipe development; OLMo 3 32B base was evaluated on RULER at 4K/8K/16K/32K/65K (Table 12).

evidence.excerpt: RULER (Hsieh et al., 2024) is a benchmark of synthetic long-context tasks … we use RULER as the primary metric to guide our long-context recipe development
evidence.explanation: Documents RULER as the development metric for the long-context base.

edges.subject: allenai/olmo-3-1125-32b
edges.description: HELMET (Yen et al., 2025) was held out as an unseen long-context evaluation suite for OLMo 3 32B base across the same context lengths (Table 12). 32B base scored 52.11 / 49.36 / 48.60 / 43.15 at 8K/16K/32K/65K.

evidence.excerpt: We keep HELMET as an unseen evaluation suite and test our final checkpoints on it.
evidence.explanation: Documents HELMET as the held-out long-context evaluation suite.
